---
title: "Second hotkey that opens in one-shot mode (default Ctrl+Shift+,) — report"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-22"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>⌨️</text></svg>">
---

# Status

Done and shipped. CI green (Build ✓, Test ✓). Plan:
[`20260722-204844_oneshot-hotkey.plan.qmd`](20260722-204844_oneshot-hotkey.plan.qmd).

# What was done

Added a second, configurable global hotkey — default `Ctrl+Shift+,` (`Oemcomma`) — that opens
the overlay in **one-shot** mode regardless of `OverlayTriggerMode` (which defaults to
`Continuous`). The main hotkey (`Ctrl+Shift+M`) still honors the configured mode. Pressing
either hotkey while the overlay is already up toggles one-click ⇄ continuous (Grid only).

- `OverlayActionConfig.cs`: pure `ComputeIsContinuous(forceOneShot, gridSource, configMode)`
  (forceOneShot wins; else Grid && config Continuous) + `ReadOneShotHotkeyKey` /
  `ReadOneShotHotkeyModifier` (mirror the main-hotkey readers; defaults `Oemcomma` /
  `Control|Shift`).
- `IKeyListenerService.cs` / `KeyListenerService.cs`: new `OneShotHotKey` slot +
  `OnOneShotHotKeyActivated` event, registered via the existing `ReRegisterHotKey` path, and a
  symmetric `WndProc` dispatch block.
- `ShellViewModel.cs`: open body refactored into `OpenOverlayAsync(bool forceOneShot)`; two
  thin `async void` handlers call it with `false` (main) / `true` (one-shot). `continuous` now
  comes from `ComputeIsContinuous`.
- `OptionsViewModel.cs` / `OptionsView.xaml`: `OneShotHotkeyKey` / `OneShotHotkeyModifier`
  rows (Keyboard tab).
- `App.config`: `OneShotHotkeyKey=Oemcomma`, `OneShotHotkeyModifier=Control,Shift` with a
  comment block.
- Tests: `ComputeIsContinuous_RespectsForceOneShotAndGrid` theory (6 cases) covers the
  forceOneShot override, Automation one-shot, and config OneClick.
- `CLAUDE.md` / `CHANGELOG.md` updated.

No new `.cs` files (all edits to existing files) → no csproj churn.

# Verification (Rule #0)

- Key code confirmed authoritative: **`Oemcomma` = 188 (0xBC)**, present in
  `netframework-4.5.1` ([Microsoft Learn — Keys Enum](
  https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.keys)).
- CI run 29921716305 (commit `b94aea4`): Build ✓, Test ✓ (55s; new
  `ComputeIsContinuous` theory + existing tests). Only annotation is the unrelated
  Node.js 20 deprecation notice.
- Shipped `hap.exe.config` verified to contain `OneShotHotkeyKey=Oemcomma` and
  `OneShotHotkeyModifier=Control,Shift`.
- Drop: `$HAP_WIN_TEST_DIR/hap-oneshot/hap.exe` →
  `/mnt/c/5.sibio/93.bk/20260627-144526.test/hap-oneshot/hap.exe`.
- Runtime (pending, Windows): `Ctrl+Shift+,` opens a one-shot overlay that closes after one
  click even when `OverlayTriggerMode=Continuous`; `Ctrl+Shift+M` still opens in the
  configured mode; pressing `Ctrl+Shift+,` while a Continuous overlay is up flips it to
  one-shot.

# Commit

`b94aea4` on `master`.
